user_message: The CSA escalated Treyarch Global to me -- can you prep a brief?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 14:09 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Treyarch Global QBR preparation

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 15:59 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Treyarch Global
Date: 2024-02-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is QBR preparation.

[00:06:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:09:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:21:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Treyarch Global: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
